identity.title: Clocked Cascaded Two-Pole Filter
identity.supported_scope: pure voltage-domain event-driven transient behavioral Verilog-A

parameters.name: gain
parameters.override_behavior: sets input-deviation gain about 0.45 V common mode

parameters.name: alpha
parameters.override_behavior: sets each cascaded sampled low-pass update coefficient

properties.observable_contract: Both cascaded states and observable out return to 0.45 V during initialization and active-high reset.
Each eligible rising edge forms a rail-bounded target from gain times the input deviation around 0.45 V.
Out follows the second of two cascaded alpha-weighted sampled low-pass states and therefore settles more slowly than a single direct update.
Metric exposes the centered lag between the two cascaded states during settling and returns toward its baseline after convergence.
The driven output remains within the public 0 V through 0.9 V signal range.

modeling_constraints: Use two deterministic cascaded rising-edge sampled low-pass states.
Do not use current contributions, ddt(), validation hooks, or simulator-specific side channels.
